validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_merge_batch.TestLockContention -v passes 6/6 (2.2s, up from 4 -- 2 new cases for unit_040); python -W error::ResourceWarning confirms clean handle management in the subprocess test; python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 206/206 (34.7s, up from 204)

checkpoint.summary: unit_040 complete. PHASE_009 CLOSED. Concurrent merge serialization proven end-to-end. PHASE_005 unit_023 already gave us 4 TestLockContention cases (two-thread contention, exception cleanup, stale-lock takeover, fresh-lock timeout) -- all still pass. unit_040 adds 2 new cases to close the 'two merge_batch.py invocations' wording in the acceptance: test_two_subprocess_acquirers_serialize_on_disk_lock spawns a Python subprocess that acquires _MergeLock, signals 'HELD' on stdout, sleeps 0.5s, releases; the main process then acquires the same lock in-process and asserts elapsed >= 0.4s, proving OS-level O_EXCL semantics hold across processes (production scenario: two CLI invocations racing from two shells/schedulers). test_lock_path_is_exactly_harness_dot_lock pins the on-disk lock location so a future LOCK_FILENAME rename cannot silently break cross-process serialization. PIPE handles explicitly closed; python -W error::ResourceWarning confirms no 3.12+ warnings leak. PHASE_009 summary: unit_037 (session kill switch, safety_rails.py + 15 tests + hook _stop() sweeps), unit_038 (batch-of-1 no-special-case doc contract + 3 grep-as-test cases), unit_039 (scope-violation always-on policy in phase-contract.md + 3 policy tests), unit_040 (cross-process merge lock + 2 subprocess tests). Suite 183 -> 204 -> 206 across the phase. pr-review-checklist: all units have validation_evidence; no linter/type errors (stdlib-only Python); codebase patterns followed; all tests pass; phase is not deploy-affecting (config.deployment.target = 'none', skill distribution repo); docs updated; checkpoint current. PHASE_009 marked completed in phase-graph.json.
